subject: Physics
question_text: Doping silicon with a pentavalent impurity (such as phosphorus) creates an n-type semiconductor. The 'n' stands for:
option_b: Negative — referring to the negative charge of the extra free electrons contributed by the donor

subject: Physics
question_text: In a p-type semiconductor, a hole is best described as:
option_a: A physical gap in the crystal lattice
option_b: A vacancy left in the valence band when an electron moves away, which behaves as a positive charge carrier
